papers.title: Death of the Author, Birth of the Scriptor
papers.subject: scholarly communication
papers.method_hint: design-gated models, reviewer simulation, claim ledgers, reproducibility checks
improvement_questions: For scholarly communication, do the data support design-gated models, reviewer simulation, claim ledgers, reproducibility checks, or should the paper stay descriptive?
What would make the paper more credible without pretending the data identify more than they do?
What would a hostile reviewer say is the largest gap between claim and evidence?
Which next data join would most change the paper's contribution?
Can another subject reuse this workflow without changing paper-specific code?

papers.title: The Shovel-Ready Trap
papers.subject: regional science
papers.method_hint: spatial models, stage conversion models, event studies, synthetic controls, causal forests when covariates justify them
improvement_questions: For regional science, do the data support spatial models, stage conversion models, event studies, synthetic controls, causal forests when covariates justify them, or should the paper stay descriptive?
Which place-level mechanism is being identified, and what spatial spillover could break the design?
What would a hostile reviewer say is the largest gap between claim and evidence?
Which next data join would most change the paper's contribution?
Can another subject reuse this workflow without changing paper-specific code?

questions: What missing data would most improve identification or construct validity?
Which estimator is justified by the observed design, and which tempting estimators should be refused?
What reviewer objection would be most damaging if left unanswered?
Which source, citation, or measurement assumption is weakest?
What would make the output easier to audit, reproduce, and reuse across another subject?
